name: Chinese: Szechwan Noodles with Green Onions
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Hot chili oil (or to taste)
6 Green onions, sliced finely on the bias
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a generous amount of unsalted water to a boil.
2. Add the noodles (all at once) and cook for approximately 2 minutes, or until al dente (toothy but firm). Use chopsticks or two wooden spoons to separate the noodles as they cook. Do not overcook them.
3. Transfer the noodles into a large colander, then immediately rinse with cold running water until cool. Shake off excess water and drain for 15 to 30 minutes.
4. In a small bowl, combine sesame oil, soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, and chili oil; mix well.
5. Pour the seasoned sauce over the noodles and use your hands to evenly distribute it. Work carefully so that the noodles do not break. Gently spread the sauce over each strand, allowing the noodles to fully absorb the sauce before eating.
6. Add all of the sliced green onions except for 2 tablespoons. Toss and mix to evenly distribute the onions.
7. Garnish with the remaining green onions, sesame seeds, and cilantro, if desired.
8. Allow the noodles to sit and let the flavors meld for a few hours before serving. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
